arts.ttl: Genoa vs. Roma: first lunchtime clash
arts.sum: After the 4-1 success in their last home match against Roma in the league, Genoa could win 2+ games in a row at the Ferraris against the Giallorossi in Serie A for the first time since 2011 (four). Since his first goal against the Giallorossi in the tournament (February 2019), only João Pedro (six) has scored more goals than him player against Roma in the league.

arts.ttl: Nicola Zalewski Faces Roma Exile After Rejecting Galatasaray Move
arts.sum: Roma wingback Nicola Zalewski has become a subject of controversy after he turned down Galatasaray's late approach. His decision to reject a move to Istanbul hasn't gone well with the Giallorossi hierarchy, as they banished him from the squad. The post Nicola Zalewski Faces Roma Exile After Rejecting Galatasaray Move appeared first on The Cult of Calcio.

arts.ttl: Zalewski’s agent defends player’s right to decline Galatasaray transfer
arts.sum: Roma midfielder Nicola Zalewski has found himself at the center of controversy after refusing a transfer to Galatasaray. The club subsequently excluded him from the squad, but Zalewski’s representatives have now spoken out in defense of the player’s decision. In a statement released through the ANSA news agency, Zalewski’s management agency GP Soccer and Management emphasized the player’s right to make his own career choices. They stated, “A player has an inalienable right to decide whether transfers are finalized or not regarding his professional activities, without being conditioned or forced.” The agency clarified that while Zalewski had initially been open The post Zalewski’s agent defends player’s right to decline Galatasaray transfer appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: Gilardino: “Let’s forget last year, I expect a Genoa-like performance”
arts.sum: Genoa manager Alberto Gilardino addressed the media in a pre-match conference ahead of his team’s upcoming fixture against Roma. The coach touched on various topics, including player fitness, team mentality, and the challenges of facing a strong opponent. Gilardino began by expressing satisfaction with his players’ international performances: “I’m happy that they played, some more than others. I’m pleased that Genoa players had the opportunity to confirm their places in their respective national teams. It’s a fact. The work of these years has enhanced the characteristics of individuals.
